Ad Text:
About MSA National

MSA National is an Australian legal and technology company and the country’s largest outsource provider of mortgage documentation and settlement services to major financial institutions. Operating from offices in Sydney, Melbourne and Brisbane, we handle high volumes of settlements daily and continue to grow. Our people, our systems and our service standards are the foundation of everything we do.


The Opportunity

AI represents the most significant strategic opportunity in front of our business this decade. We are establishing a dedicated AI function, sitting outside of technology and reporting directly to the Executive, to drive this agenda with the rigour and focus it deserves.

As Head of AI & Analytics, you will own the strategy, the use-case portfolio, the adoption metrics and the executive-level narrative. You will also hold accountability for our data and analytics foundations — the infrastructure that underpins effective AI deployment. This is a strategic and commercial leadership role: we have engineering capability for the build; we need you for the thinking, the prioritisation and the orchestration.

You will work alongside our AI Change Lead, who manages the people side of the programme, and report directly to the Executive, providing regular reporting on where adoption is progressing, where value is being realised and where the business should focus next.


What You'll Be Doing

•       Own the AI strategy for MSA National — determining what we build, what we procure, what we partner on, and in what sequence

•       Lead the AI function: surface use cases from across the business, prioritise them against value and risk, and manage them from concept through to production

•       Maintain governance oversight of all AI tools and automation in use across the business, ensuring visibility, monitoring and accountability

•       Own our data warehouse, business intelligence reporting and analytics roadmap; ensure our AI programme has the data foundations it requires

•       Partner with the AI Change Lead on staff training, AI tool rollout and internal capability-building

•       Partner with IT, Risk and the CISO on deployment governance, risk controls and responsible AI practices

•       Evaluate and run proof-of-concept engagements with AI vendors and partners, bringing commercial rigour to selection decisions

•       Present regular AI programme reporting to the Executive team, covering adoption, use cases in progress, value delivered and emerging risks

•       Monitor the external AI landscape and provide the Executive with timely intelligence on developments relevant to our business and sector





What You'll Bring

Essential

•       7+ years in a senior analytics, data or AI leadership role, ideally within a mid-sized financial services, legal services or shared-services business

•       A genuinely strategic mindset — experienced in portfolio prioritisation and connecting AI investment to measurable business outcomes

•       Deep AI fluency — able to engage credibly with engineers on predictive, generative and agentic AI, while operating primarily as a strategist and orchestrator rather than a practitioner

•       Strong commercial instincts — you connect every use case back to cost-to-serve, revenue, speed or customer experience

•       Hands-on familiarity with data infrastructure — data warehouses, BI platforms and a sound understanding of data quality requirements

•       Exceptional communication skills — equally effective briefing an executive team, authoring a policy document and engaging a frontline team

•       Experience managing external vendor and partner relationships, including proof-of-concept to production processes


Desirable:

•       Hands-on experience building and deploying AI agents or automation tools

•       Background in mortgage, conveyancing, legal or PEXA-adjacent businesses

•       Completion of a structured AI leadership programme

•       Prior experience establishing an AI or analytics function from the ground up




 What We Offer

•        A senior leadership role with direct Executive sponsorship and broad organisational visibility

•       The opportunity to design and build the AI function from first principles, rather than inherit an existing one

•       An Executive team that is genuinely committed to the AI agenda and actively engaged in its success

•       Modern Macquarie Park offices and the scale of a national business

•       Competitive base salary and short-term incentive, a professional development budget, and a clear mandate to reshape how we operate


How to Apply

Please apply via SEEK with your CV and a brief cover note. We would welcome your reflections on one AI or analytics initiative you are particularly proud of, and how you measured its impact.

Shortlisted candidates will meet the CEO, then our Head of Operations and CIO, then a small panel from the Executive team. We aim to have the successful candidate in the role within 30 to 45 days.


General Guidelines

All enquiries will be treated in the strictest confidence. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. This is strictly a full-time, work from office position.

About the job

12-Month Fixed Term Contract
Brisbane CBD, Flexible Working
Up to $180,000 + Super
The Company

A large organisation in the financial services space is undertaking a renewed phase of an investment performance uplift and is seeking an experienced Investment Performance & Power BI Specialist to play a key role in strengthening investment reporting, analytics, and data‑driven insight.

This is a high‑impact, 12‑month fixed term contract suited to a technically strong middle‑office investment reporting professional, with deep Power BI capability and hands‑on experience delivering investment performance reporting within asset management or superannuation environments.

The Opportunity

You will support the investment function by designing, building, and delivering high‑quality performance reporting and dashboards across diversified portfolios. The role has a strong emphasis on Power BI development, performance analytics, and data accuracy, sitting at the intersection of investments, risk, compliance, and executive reporting.

This position is critical to uplifting how investment performance, attribution, exposure, and analytics are delivered, with a clear focus on reporting, transparency, and insight rather than front‑office portfolio management.

Key Responsibilities
Build, develop, and maintain Power BI dashboards from scratch for investment and executive reporting
Deliver accurate, timely investment performance and attribution reporting across multi‑asset portfolios
Enhance performance analytics frameworks, including exposure analysis, drawdowns, and scenario analysis
Translate complex investment data into clear, decision‑ready insights for senior stakeholders
Support asset exposure monitoring, portfolio insights, and performance commentary
Conduct quantitative analysis including trend analysis, scenario modelling, and drawdown analytics
Work closely with custodians and data providers to reconcile, validate, and improve data quality
Partner with stakeholders across Investments, Risk, Compliance, and Finance
Contribute to automation and continuous improvement initiatives within the investment reporting function
About You

We are targeting a profile with strong alignment to the following:

Proven investment performance reporting experience
Strong background within asset management, or institutional investment environments
Hands‑on, advanced Power BI development capability (dashboard build, data modelling, automation)
Experience working in middle‑office investment reporting, analytics, or performance teams
Exposure to multi‑asset portfolios (equities, fixed income, alternatives)
Strong data handling skills (SQL and Excel essential; Python highly regarded)
Solid understanding of investment performance metrics, attribution methodologies, and exposure analysis
If this sounds like your next contract opportunity, please hit “APPLY” now.
For more information, contact Sam Bott on sam.bott@perigongroup.com.au

About O’Brien® Glass 

O’Brien® Glass is one of Australia’s most trusted household brands, known for delivering exceptional service and quality for over 100 years. With a national footprint and a strong commitment to innovation, we’re modernising the way we use data to support smarter decision making across the business. When you join O’Brien®, you join a team driven by values, collaboration, and a genuine focus on helping our people succeed. 




About the Role 

We’re looking for an experienced Data Engineer to join our Business Intelligence team on an initial 12-month fixed term basis and help shape the future of data at O’Brien® Glass. 

In this role, you will design, build, and maintain reliable end-to-end data pipelines and models that support accurate, timely, and trusted insights across the organisation. 

Working closely with the Business Intelligence Manager, you’ll play a key role in improving data quality, automating core processes, and lifting our overall data capability. 




What You’ll Be Doing 

Building and maintaining automated data pipelines, transformations, and reporting solutions 

Ensuring data accuracy and reliability through rigorous testing and validation 

Analysing complex business and financial data to support decision-making 

Troubleshooting data issues and supporting continuous improvement initiatives 

Partnering with IT, BI, and business stakeholders, along with external consultants 




What You’ll Bring 

4–5 years’ experience in Data Engineering or BI 

Strong SQL, ETL/ELT, and data modelling expertise 

Excellent communication and documentation skills 

Strong business acumen and attention to detail 

Bachelor degree and/or relevant vendor certifications 

Nice to Have: 

Snowflake certification 

Experience with Finance, Supply Chain, Service Management, or Call Centre systems 




Why You’ll Love Working at O’Brien® Glass 

Be part of an exciting transformation of our data environment 

Supportive and collaborative BI and IT teams 

Opportunities to influence data processes and deliver meaningful business value 

Work for a trusted Australian brand with strong values and a people-first culture 




Ready to make an impact? Apply now and help shape the future of data at O’Brien® Glass. 




If you meet the above criteria and you’re seeking a rewarding career with a nationally recognised household brand, then we would love to hear from you. Apply Now!




At O'Brien®, we put our customer at the heart of everything that we do, serving more than 360,000 Customers each year, 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, 365 days a year. O'Brien is also part of Belron®, worldwide leader in vehicle glass repair, replacement and recalibration services, operating in 40 countries across 6 continents

Privacy: To review and consider your application, O'Brien® collects personal information. To understand what is collected and why, carefully review the Applicant Privacy Notice presented during your online application and/or review our Privacy Policy and Terms of Use.

Agencies: O'Brien® does not accept unsolicited resumes or outreach from recruiting agencies. Absent a signed agreement and approval from O'Brien® to submit candidates to a specific requisition, we will not approve payment to any third party.

About DBG Health
At DBG Health, we are Defined by Good. Guided by a simple but powerful purpose: to create a world where feeling better is a right, not a privilege. Every day, millions of people rely on us for access to affordable, innovative health, wellness, and beauty solutions that make a genuine impact. Headquartered in Melbourne, we are a global-scale, privately owned organisation, powered by over 1,500 passionate professionals, united by a mission to help people feel better, live well, and thrive. 
  
The Operations division brings together Information Technology, Supply Chain, Finance, the Project Management Office, and Scientific Affairs to support and enable all key divisions across the group. Operating at both strategic and operational levels, our operations team ensure seamless systems, robust governance, efficient supply chains, and scientific integrity, empowering DBG Health’s divisions to perform at their best and deliver exceptional outcomes for customers, partners, and patients alike.
 
About The Role:
 
 As a Senior Data Engineer, you'll design, build, and optimise the data pipelines and platforms that power analytics and decision-making across the organisation. Working across the full data lifecycle, from ingestion and transformation to modelling and storage, you'll ensure systems are reliable, scalable, and high-performing.
 
You'll collaborate with engineers, architects, and business stakeholders to deliver modern data solutions, champion best practices in data quality and governance, and mentor junior team members. You'll also enable secure, reliable access to trusted data for analysts, data scientists, and business users, driving innovation and operational excellence across the organisation.
 
Role & Responsibilities:

Lead the design, build, and optimisation of scalable ETL/ELT pipelines using Azure Data Factory, Synapse Analytics, and Data Lake, ensuring reliability, security, automation, and performance.
Translate complex business requirements into robust, cloud-native solutions, providing technical guidance, reviewing designs, and promoting reusable, standardised patterns.
Work with business stakeholders, product owners, and analysts to deliver fit-for-purpose data solutions and act as a trusted advisor on accessing and leveraging data.
Implement and enforce governance, lineage, and quality frameworks, partnering with security and compliance teams to meet privacy, risk, and regulatory standards.
Evaluate emerging Azure services, identify opportunities to optimise workflows, and lead proof-of-concepts to enhance performance, reduce costs, and uplift data engineering maturity.
Oversee the health of pipelines, databases, and environments, support project delivery teams, and provide expert input to ensure smooth adoption of new data services.
Skills & Experience:

5-8 years' experience designing and optimising ETL/ELT pipelines, enterprise data warehouses, and BI platforms; proven ability to translate complex business strategies into scalable, high-performance solutions.
Deep proficiency with Microsoft technologies including SQL Server, Azure Data Factory, Azure Data Lake, and Azure Synapse Analytics; strong knowledge of dimensional and relational data modelling (data vault, star schema).
Advanced analytical, problem-solving, and logical reasoning skills; demonstrated ability to influence and collaborate with senior stakeholders.
Desirable: Experience with Python, Spark, Databricks, CI/CD pipelines, infrastructure as code, and automated deployment; exposure to multi-cloud or hybrid architectures.
Experience leading or mentoring data engineering teams, enforcing coding standards, and supporting Agile/Scrum delivery practices is highly regarded.
Tertiary qualification in Computer Science, Data Engineering, BI, IT, or related field; relevant Microsoft certifications (e.g., DP-203, DP-900, DP-300, AZ-305) and additional cloud, DevOps, or security certifications are advantageous.
Benefits   

Exclusive employee discounts via the DBG Health Employee Shop, including MCoBeauty, Esmi Skin Minerals, Nude by Nature, e.l.f. Cosmetics, Chemist's Own and more.  
Acacia EAP Wellbeing Support, providing confidential 24/7 counselling, mental health support, financial coaching, and manager assistance.  
Join a values-driven, supportive, and friendly culture within a dynamic, fast-growing business that actively promotes professional development and internal mobility.  
Enjoy the ability to work autonomously in an empowering environment that supports ownership, accountability and personal success.  
Access to the Bastas Academy for Health Leadership (via Melbourne Business School), supporting emerging and senior leaders with industry-leading programs, coaching, and development.  
DBG Health Leadership Academy, offering structured leadership development, mentoring, and clear career progression pathways.    
DBG Health is committed to fostering a workplace culture that is safe and accessible for all. We are an equal- opportunity employer and encourage applications regardless of cultural background, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability or neurodiversity. We strive to build a collaborative, inclusive workplace that is representative of the communities we work and live in.

Job Title: Head of business improvement (Transformation & continuous improvement)
Ad Text:
This exciting newly created Head of business improvement opportunity, will play a key role within a well established company with a global presence.

This role will be responsible for driving enterprise-wide transformation and continuous improvement agenda, ensuring that global business processes are efficient, customer-focused, and fit for purpose.
This role leads the design, delivery, and embedding of initiatives that enhance operational performance, simplify systems, and build a culture of excellence and innovation.
The role provides specialist expertise in process improvement and change enablement. It collaborates across Corporate Services, Product, and Shared Services to deliver measurable improvements in efficiency, quality, and customer experience, while ensuring alignment with the company strategic goals and values.

Detail on scope include;

Leads the planning and delivery of business improvement and transformation initiatives that align with the enterprise strategy.
Identifies opportunities for process optimisation and efficiency gains
Applies Lean Six Sigma (or similar) and structured problem-solving methods to identify pain points and implement solutions that deliver measurable value.
Oversees documentation of current (“as is”) and future (“to be”) processes and ensures the realisation of defined business benefits.
Monitor and report on the progress of transformation initiatives against established goals.
Manage change effectively, addressing resistance and ensuring stakeholder engagement.
Utilise data and analytics to drive decisions making and measure the impact of improvements
Builds strong partnerships with stakeholders across regions and functions to identify opportunities, gather feedback, and align improvement priorities.
Facilitates workshops and planning sessions that capture business needs and ensure effective engagement throughout the change journey
Embeds a culture of continuous improvement and operational excellence.
Establishes and maintains frameworks and tools for consistent delivery of improvement initiatives.
Analyses process performance data to identify trends, opportunities, and quick wins.
Champions innovation through pilots and process automation initiatives
Experience/Skills 

Skills

Proven experience delivering large-scale business improvement or transformation projects in complex, multi-stakeholder environments.
Expertise in Lean Six Sigma, process design, and performance measurement.
Experience in Technology space and supporting implementation or optimisation and/or BPO initiatives.
Strong analytical, facilitation, and stakeholder-management skills.
Proficiency in project management methodologies and tools.
Strong business acumen with ability to link operational change to strategic outcomes.
This is a fantastic opportunity, offering a lot of autonomy, support from the business and Executive's, as well company culture/values held in high regard

Senior Data Analyst

Work with iconic brands including Caroma and Methven
Be the link between business teams and data, owning insights and reporting
North Sydney / Permanent role / Flexibility to split time between home and office




This role is a hands‑on Senior Data Analyst position, where you translate business questions into clear, practical insights for well‑known brands like Caroma and Methven.


In this role, you will:

Own business insights and reporting
Act as the bridge between business users and technical data teams
Turn business needs into clear reporting
Build dashboards in Power BI
Write and optimise SQL queries
Prioritise reporting by business impact


The role

GWA Group is home to some of Australia and New Zealand's most recognised kitchen and bathroom brands, including Caroma, Methven, Dorf and Clark.

This role sits between the business and the data team and plays a key part in how information is used across GWA Group. You'll work directly with stakeholders across Sales, Marketing, Supply Chain and Finance to understand what they need from data, why they need it and how it will be used, partnering closely with data engineers to deliver meaningful, usable insights.


About you

You enjoy working with data and people.

You may come from a data or analytics background, or from a commercial role where you've developed strong analytical capability. What matters most is your ability to translate data into language the business understands and can act on.


You are likely to bring:

Strong experience with Power BI
Solid SQL skills and hands‑on reporting experience
Confidence engaging with non‑technical stakeholders
A commercial mindset and curiosity around why data matters
A structured, organised approach to prioritising work
Experience with Microsoft Fabric or modern data platforms is a bonus, but not essential.


Why join GWA Group

At GWA Group, our brands including Caroma and Methven are used in homes across Australia and New Zealand every day.

This role offers exposure across a large, complex organisation, real influence over how data is used, long‑term career opportunity and a collaborative, down‑to‑earth culture.

Work options: Hybrid



Wesfarmers OneDigital is building Australia's leading data and digital retail ecosystem, underpinned by AI across everything we do. We operate across four pillars - loyalty, data, retail media and AI, turning millions of customer interactions into insights that shape how Australians shop, live and connect.

We’re focused on harnessing the power of data and transforming it into meaningful insights that enhance our customer experience, strengthen our businesses, and help them pursue new growth opportunities. Our talented and agile team co-create and solve complex problems. It’s innovation, to create better experiences for our customers. It’s adding real value, that supports decision making across the Wesfarmers Group, and its transformational work, that’ll change the industries we operate in.

In your role as a Data Analyst you will be part of a team leveraging our shared data asset, which is one of Australia’s largest retail data sets. Your role will support Wesfarmers divisions to leverage this asset, to drive tangible commercial value by understanding our customers and improving the way we engage with them. You will need to be comfortable digging into the details of Wesfarmers data and finding the key insights that really drive our business forward.

 

What you’ll be doing

•     Identify the key actionable insights through the analysis of data and translate this into clear business relevant recommendations.

•     Apply appropriate leading practice analytical methods and techniques in an agile, pragmatic way that reflects the needs, constraints and KPIs of the business (e.g. time, cost, quality, data availability).

•     Create business enthusiasm and demand for the use of all types of analytics through the effective communication and translation of analytical techniques into commercial concepts that can be easily understood by business end users.

•     Create a collaborative relationship with stakeholders with effective articulation of the customer opportunity, commercial benefit, analytical findings and data driven insight recommendations.

•     Showcase, benchmark and quantify the value and outcomes of analytics-based decision making.

•     Contribute to the direction, development and implementation of the data and analytics strategy, roadmap and knowledge base within the Corporate Advanced Analytics and Divisional analytics teams.

•     Serve as a trusted advisor by cultivating relationships with stakeholders and across various Leadership teams to deliver ad-hoc insights and by building repeatable reports and so the value from becoming a data driven organisation.

•     Contribute to OnePass’ ability to delivery strategic and timely analytics

 

What you'll bring to the table

•    Analytics professional with a proven track record in the development and business use of digital analytics technology and working with large & complex data sets

•    Track record in designing and delivering data driven and/or analytics solutions that are actionable

•    Ability to engage and influence business users in the opportunity and benefit from data driven decisions

•    A real and demonstrable interest in analytics and its use and exploitation in a business context

•    Familiarity with web architecture including tag management, data layers, analytics tracking, and corresponding data flows

•    Experience with digital analytics and tag management tools (Google Analytics/Adobe/Tealium)

•    Retail Industry experience will be highly regarded

•    Experience in big data analytics and technology preferred

•    Proficiency in SQL and/or other analytics languages

•    Well-developed interpersonal and influencing skills

•    Business and commercial acumen

•    Bachelor or Masters degree in a Computer Science, Econometrics, Accounting, Applied Statistics, Engineering, Marketing, or another numerate field




Key Team Member Benefits

To support you at work, what you value in life and in the community, our team member benefits include:

Fantastic career and development opportunities within your role and across a diverse range of businesses within the Group
Convenient city location
Modern and spacious end of trip facilities
Supported Learning opportunities
Paid primary carer parental leave (16 weeks) and non-primary carer parental leave (4 weeks)
Paid leave to volunteer within the community
Hybrid working – we balance working together with working flexibly
A team member discount card and discounts across a range of businesses – including Bunnings, Kmart, Target, Officeworks, Clearskin Care and Kleenheat Gas
Free OnePass membership

Next steps

If this sounds like your next career move, then click on the ‘Apply’ button now! Please note that we may commence interviewing candidates prior to the application closing date.

Company: Victorian Legal Services Board and Commissioner
Ad Text:
Can you tell me more about what you do at VLSB+C?

Great question! We're really proud of the work we do. We are the regulator of the Victorian legal profession. We license lawyers and oversee service standards and conduct. This includes handling complaints about lawyers, investigating poor conduct and overseeing management of trust accounts. We are also the stewards of the public purpose and fidelity funds and administer these to support legal regulation and access to justice in Victoria.

We are a modern best practice regulator, using data and insights to help identify where our actions can have the greatest impact on ensuring ethical and professional legal practice by Victorian lawyers. We are passionate about maintaining and protecting the rule of law by ensuring the integrity of the legal profession.

Okay, and what role are you recruiting for?

We're looking for a Principal Evaluation Adviser to join our Evaluation & Impact team. You will provide thought leadership on evaluation and outcomes measurement, strengthens organisational capability, and ensures that evidence and insights meaningfully inform planning, strategy and continuous improvement.

This is getting interesting, what type of person are you looking for?

We are looking for candidates with the following:

Extensive experience leading evaluation and learning activities, including the design and delivery of process, outcome and impact evaluations
Demonstrated capability in qualitative and quantitative analysis, with experience using data reporting systems, evaluation frameworks and other tools to generate strategic insights for decision‑makers
Demonstrated experience working in consulting or client‑focused environments, with the ability to build trusted relationships, manage multiple concurrent projects, and navigate organisational priorities and dynamics to deliver high‑quality advice and outputs
Tertiary qualifications in evaluation, social research, policy, behavioural science

That sounds like me! How are you different from other organisations?

We're really excited by our organisation, we offer:

Flexibility: We have work leading edge flexible working options including part-time, compressed hours, flexible start time, up to 2 days working from home.
Culture: Our People Matter Survey results reflect our staff are proud to work for the VLSB+C.
Wellbeing: We have an active Social Club and Health and Wellbeing Committee that promote fun and engaging activities throughout the year
Partnership: our horizontal structure allows for more partnership with your colleagues and other teams
Purpose: We play a key role in regulating the legal practice in Victoria

I want to know more!

Taimur Siddiqi would love to hear from you on 03 9679 8115 if you have questions about the role.

I'm now really interested!

Great! Our recruitment is all about red carpet not red tape so simply click on ‘Apply Now' before midnight 10 May. We can't wait to hear from you!

Other relevant information:

Appointment will be subject to abiding by our Flexible Working Procedure which defines the number of required in-office days at our office (including the ‘team day' of your team) and completion of the VPS Pre-Employment Statutory Declaration, Misconduct Declaration and Consent Form and the Pre-Employment Health Declaration.

Your appointment may be subject to a satisfactory National Police Record Check, provision of evidence of qualifications required for your position and/or a probation period.

Senior Analyst – Asset Management

Bondi Junction | Full-Time

Deliver commercially focused analysis and modelling to support performance and growth.

 

Gunn Agri Partners was established in 2013 and provides agricultural investment and farm management services for Australian, North American and European investors. 

Gunn Agri Partners manages over $1.2bn of assets and commitments managed across five investment platforms. Gunn Agri manages agriculture assets Australia wide in mixed farming, grains, grass fed protein, tree nuts, stone fruit and natural capital.

We are now seeking a Senior Analyst – Asset Management to support investment performance across our mixed farming and natural capital portfolios, partnering closely with asset managers and senior stakeholders to deliver high-quality financial analysis, modelling and insights.

About the Role

As Senior Analyst – Asset Management, you will play a key role in supporting the performance and development of Gunn Agri’s operating platforms.

This role is responsible for delivering robust financial modelling, performance analysis and reporting across a diverse portfolio of agricultural and natural capital assets. You will work closely with the team to provide insights that support investment decisions, capital allocation and operational performance.

You’ll balance detailed analytical work with stakeholder engagement, contributing to investment cases, reporting frameworks and strategic initiatives. Working collaboratively with asset managers, finance and senior leadership, you’ll help drive data-led decision-making and continuous improvement across the portfolio.

Based in Bondi Junction, this role suits a commercially-minded analyst with strong modelling capability and a proactive, solutions-focused approach.

Key Responsibilities

Investment Analysis & Modelling • Develop and maintain detailed financial models (including integrated three-way models) • Support investment analysis across acquisitions, divestments and asset management initiatives • Conduct scenario and sensitivity analysis to support capital allocation decisions • Prepare investment papers, summaries and recommendations

Performance Analysis & Reporting • Analyse key drivers of financial, operational and natural capital performance • Build and enhance reporting frameworks across assets and platforms • Extract, consolidate and interpret data to deliver actionable insights • Identify trends, risks and opportunities to support decision-making

Project Delivery & Stakeholder Engagement • Contribute to due diligence and investment-related projects • Collaborate with internal teams and external stakeholders to support outcomes • Build strong working relationships across asset management, finance and operations.

About You

You are a commercially minded analyst who enjoys working with data to drive real-world outcomes.

You will bring: • 3–5+ years’ experience in an analytical, investment or financial role • Strong financial modelling capability (advanced Excel essential) • Experience in corporate, investment or professional services environments • Ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights • Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills • High attention to detail with a proactive and curious mindset

Experience within agriculture, agribusiness, natural capital or asset management will be highly regarded.

Why Join Gunn Agri Partners?

Purpose with Impact – Contribute to sustainable agriculture and natural capital outcomes Values-Led Culture – Excellence, Teamwork, Community, Integrity and Sustainability Exposure & Growth – Work across diverse assets and investment platforms Collaborative Environment – Partner with experienced asset managers and leadership Competitive Benefits – Including salary sacrifice, purchase leave, parental leave, EAP, novated leasing and more

This is an excellent opportunity to step into a high-impact role within a growing, purpose-driven asset management business.

Apply now or reach out for a confidential discussion. Join a business investing in people, land and long-term outcomes.

Job Title: Vice President, Fixed Asset Advisory Services
Ad Text:
In a world of disruption and increasingly complex business challenges, our professionals bring truth into focus with the Kroll Lens. Our sharp analytical skills, paired with the latest technology, allow us to give our clients clarity—not just answers— in all areas of business. We embrace diverse backgrounds and global perspectives, and we cultivate diversity by respecting, including, and valuing one another. As part of One team, One Kroll, you’ll contribute to a supportive and collaborative work environment that empowers you to excel. 

Our Fixed Asset Advisory Services (FAAS) practice provides fixed asset valuations to a diverse portfolio of global clients.

At Kroll, your work will help deliver clarity to our clients’ most complex governance, risk, and transparency challenges. Apply now to join One team, One Kroll. 

RESPONSIBILITIES:

1. Global methodology & cost alignment

Drive alignment and standardisation of valuation and cost modelling methodologies across multiple geographies, business units and engagement types.

Reconcile and consolidate disparate cost-related data sources that exist across the business, establishing a single, coherent framework for cost benchmarking that aligns with global requirements.


2. Tech-enabled process automation 

Lead the design, development and deployment of AI-enabled tools that automate and accelerate the valuation process modelling lifecycle, from data ingestion and cost estimation through to final reporting.

Work cross-functionally with Kroll's technology and data teams (with technical support available) to specify requirements, test prototypes and scale proven tools across the practice.

Leverage generative AI, machine learning and advanced analytics to embed intelligence into valuation workflows, reducing manual effort and enhancing analytical rigour.

Champion a culture of digital innovation within the Fixed Assets practice, identifying opportunities where technology can materially improve quality, consistency and profitability of engagements.


3. Data Strategy & Structured Dataset Development

Unlock the value of Kroll's extensive historical engagement dataset by leading the conversion of unstructured asset data — accumulated across prior mandates globally — into clean, structured and queryable datasets.

Integrate structured datasets into active valuation engagements to improve cost benchmarking, market-rate assumptions and analytical depth, reducing reliance on external data sources.

Identify patterns, trends and insights from aggregated engagement data to support knowledge development, proposal writing and thought leadership.


4. People leadership & capability building

Upskilling team members by driving tech-enablement across the business 

REQUIREMENTS:

Significant experience in valuation, fixed assets, cost modelling or a related advisory discipline.

Demonstrated track record of leading process improvement, automation or data‑led transformation initiatives.

Ad Text:
The role 

Reporting to the Director, Service Management and improvement, the Reporting and Analysis Manager is responsible for delivering customer insights to eHealth Queensland staff and identifying performance insights that promote end-to-end improvement in eHealth Queensland's service delivery to its customers.

The Reporting and Analysis Manager leads a specialist team of Business Intelligence and Performance Reporting experts, managing and setting the direction of current and future activities. The manager adds value through extensive knowledge of Business Intelligence and Performance Reporting, coupled with extensive knowledge of the operations of eHealth Queensland and its customers, to implement value-adding performance reporting and analysis solutions based on industry standards and best practices. They are expected to possess leadership skills to bring together multi-disciplined teams to embed performance reporting and analysis into service operations as well as providing peer mentoring, guidance and internal development. The role will work broadly across the Enterprise Technology Services branch plus other areas of eHealth, with varied stakeholders and customers.

Key responsibilities:

Manage the core eHealth Queensland customer intelligence and service performance reporting and analysis operational processes ensuring that they meet the needs of eHealth Queensland's customers and support teams
Measure and report outcomes and performance against defined metrics
Optimise toolsets to standardise and automate processes as much as possible
Lead and manage a team of Business Intelligence and Performance Reporting specialists, instilling a culture of customer-first, continual improvement and analytical best practice and innovation
Develop and maintain the framework for service delivery performance reporting and analysis, ensuring that the process provides trustworthy data and timely delivery of information and analysis to reduce the delays in decision-making processes caused by complex or disparate manual processes, and provides end-to-end insight across eHealth Queensland's services

About you

We are searching for someone who:

Demonstrated knowledge and experience of the service management capabilities of ServiceNow, the SQL Server toolset as it supports best practice data management, specifically in a data warehousing environment, and Power BI to provide information that supports effective decision-making.
Demonstrated high level understanding of Business Intelligence and Performance Reporting frameworks and best practice, as they apply to a large ICT customer service delivery organisation
Demonstrated ability to design, implement and promote effective Business Intelligence and Performance Reporting frameworks and solutions within a large, complex service delivery organisation
Advanced interpersonal, communication, negotiation, consultation and collaboration skills incorporating the ability to liaise and consult effectively with personnel at all levels within and external to eHealth Queensland
Demonstrated high level organisational skills including the ability to establish own work priorities and manage time effectively, whilst remaining flexible to respond to changing needs in a busy environment
High level oral and written communication skills, including the ability to compile complex reports, briefs, correspondence and submissions on behalf of the Director, Service Management and Improvement
Demonstrated high level research, analytical, conceptual and problem solving skills including the proven capacity to initiate appropriate strategic and operational responses to key issues affecting the enterprise
Highly developed stakeholder engagement and management skills with the ability to build strong relationships with both internal and external stakeholders
Demonstrated ability to lead, manage and mentor staff committed to the delivery of a diverse and dynamic work program to achieve positive service outcomes for customers while effectively managing conflicting demands and tight time frames
